title: Cajun Red Beans and Rice
steps: Soak, then cook the beans. Then cook the rice.

Heat 2 pans. In one pan, cook sausages. In the other pan, saute celery, onion, green pepper. Once the peppers and onions have cooked for a few minutes, add garlic, bay leaves, veggie broth, seasonings, tomato paste, and beans. If your beans are already cooked through, wait to put them in until later.
Simmer for at least 15-20 minutes and serve! Garnish with parsley if you have it.

title: Roasted Tomato Caper Pasta
ingredientList: however many you want! (at least a few tablespoons)
My fave pasta options are brown rice, einkhorn, and lentil pasta
as much as you can get
Thyme - fresh if possible! Dried okay
a few springs if fresh or a few sprinkles if dry
steps: Preheat oven to 400. Cut tomatoes into rounds. Coat tomatoes in avocado or other high heat oil. Sprinkle with garlic powder and salt. Cover in thyme sprigs or dried thyme if fresh not available. Put tomatoes into oven and roast for minimum 20 minutes but longer is better for more flavaaaah.
Cook pasta.
Make lemon-garlic sauce: Juice the lemons. Peel the garlic. Then blend lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, red chili flakes, and a big pinch of salt using an immersion blender.
Toast pine nuts lightly (optional) and finely chop basil.

When tomatoes have finished roasting and pasta has finished cooking, put it all together. Take a bowl - put pasta in it. Top with tomatoes, capers, lemon-garlic sauce, pine nuts, basil, and vegan parm (optional).

title: Creamy White Bean Soup
ingredientList: 5ish cups (plus a little extra to help you get the texture you want)
Cashew Milk (or non-dairy milk of choice)
steps: If you haven't already cooked the beans, cook 'em up, or you can use canned beans.

Roughly chop potatoes, carrots, celery, kale, and mince garlic (They're going to get blended up later on so they don't need to look pretty - the smaller you chop them the faster they'll cook so keep that in mind).
Get large soup pot. Heat on medium heat.
Oil the soup pot and toss in the potatoes, carrots, celery and saute for a few minutes.
Add garlic cloves, onion powder, majoram, red pepper flakes and saute for another minute.
Add kale, bay leaves, and veggie broth. Bring to a boil. Once boiling, lower down to a simmer and simmer for 10 minutes.

Add in white beans and simmer for another 5 minutes. At this point, the soup should be ready to blend: you'll want to test the veggies to make sure they're soft enough - make sure the potatoes/carrots, celery can be easily pierced with a fork. If you need to, simmer the soup longer.

When veggies are soft enough to blend, remove the bay leaves. Lower the heat. Add cashew milk. Get immersion blender and blend soup. If you want to thin out soup you can always add more veggie broth at this point.
Top it off with salt, pepper, and any fresh herbs you have on hand (like oregano or parsley...nom nom).

title: THE BEST Cheez Sauce with White Beans and Cashews
ingredientList: depending on your flavor profile you might want smoked paprika, regular paprika, rosemary, thyme, lemon pepper, red pepper flakes, etc
a small spoonful - don't overdo it since the flavor will take over
steps: Soak and cook your white beans (skip this step if you're using canned beans)
Soak cashews for 4 hours in room temp water OR if you're short on time put the cashews in HOT water for 1 hour
Chop onion and garlic - saute on medium heat
Juice the lemon
Add everything to blender and blend it up! Adjust seasoning to taste.
prepTime: Several hours (in order to soak beans and cashews)
notes: This is great on anything to give it umami cheesy flavor. Try drizzling it on cabbage steaks (and add some shiitake mushroom bacon for extra goodness)

title: Soba Noodle Shiitake Green Bean Bowl with Tahini Sauce
steps: Make sauce: Blend together (using immersion blender) tahini, rice vinegar, miso, tamari, and sriracha. If too thick, add a splash of water.
Chop mushrooms. Cut stems off green beans. Chop scallions.

For mushrooms: grab a large pan and heat on medium heat. For green beans: grab a pot (with a steamer basket) and place 1 inch water in pot, start to bring to a boil. For soba: grab another pot and fill with enough water to cook the pasta, start to bring to a boil.
Splash a little high heat oil into large pan - cook the mushrooms. Once boiling, place steamer basket on pot - steam the green beans. Once boiling, cook the soba in your other pot.
When soba is finished cooking - drain it and then drizzle a little toasted sesame oil over to give it flavor and to help prevent the noodles from sticking.
Put soba in a big bowl, then cover it with the steamed green beans, the cooked shiitake mushrooms, the raw chopped scallions, the tahini sauce, and sesame seeds.
notes: This recipe is based off of this one - https://www.sakara.com/blogs/mag/daydreamer-soba-bowl

title: Cauliflower Sushi Bowls
steps: Rice the cauliflower in food processor
Chop red pepper, cucumber, avocado
Make orange sauce - juice the orange, then blend it with the tomatoes and red pepper flakes
Lightly steam the cauliflower in steamer basket (don't overdo it!)
Scoop cauliflower into bowls, top with chopped veggies and orange sauce, season with soy sauce/coconut aminos/sriracha to taste, garnish with nori sheets

title: Sweet Potato Gnocchi
steps:
Place sweet potatoes in large pot of water.
Bring to a boil.
Boil for 30 minutes or until soft (you can pierce it with a fork easily)
While the sweet potatoes are boiling, set aside 1 2/3 of flour. use remaining flour to heavily dust a large cutting board a large baking sheet
Also while sweet potatoes are boiling by combining the ground flaxseed with 3 tablespoons of water. Whisk together with a fork and set aside.
Also while sweet potatoes are boiling- get an extra large bowl and coat the (inside of the) bowl with olive oil
When sweet potatoes are finished cooking, remove from water and peel immediately
Place the peeled sweet potatoes in a kitchen aid and mash. then let them cool.
While sweet potatoes cool- prep a side dish and bring a large pot of water to a ****gentle**** boil.
Once the sweet potatoes have cooled, add in the flour, salt, and flaxegg. knead the mixture for around 4 minutes (not too long or it becomes elastic)

Start breaking off portions of dough and roll it into a thick rope (using cutting board with flour on it). cut the rope into 1/2"-1" pieces and place on (also flour dusted) baking sheet. Do this until all the dough has been used. Then take 20 minutes and put into the ****gently**** boiling water **with a spoon** and stir gently so gnocchi doesn't stick to the bottom. You'll know it's done when the gnocchi floats to the top. Scoop the gnocchi out and place into oiled bowl.
*optional: after you take out your first batch of gnocchi, put them in warm oven to keep warm.

title: Cookie Dough Bitles
ingredientList: a few spoonfuls (to roll the bites in)
a few spoonfuls (to roll the bites in)
steps: In a big bowl, combine almond butter, maple syrup, coconut flour, cacao nibs, flaxseed meal, vanilla extract, and salt to make the dough.
Roll the dough into little balls, then roll the balls in the hemp seeds/coconut shreds to give them a texture/coating.
Juice the lemon and stir it up with the yogurt. Drizzle the cookie dough bites in the lemon/yogurt frosting. (If you want them to be a little sweeter, add stevia or other sweetener to the frosting)
Put the balls in the fridge and keep them cold for at least one hour before eating. If you want to speed up the process, put them in the freezer but don't forget to take them out after 20 minutes.

title: Strawberry Arugula Salad
steps: Preheat oven to 350. Melt coconut oil. Chop the nuts and cover them in coconut oil, cinnamon, cayenne, and maple syrup. dice the red onion and chop the green onion. also chop the strawberries
Now oven is to 350 and nuts are coated - roast them for 8 minutes. Use parchment paper for god sake.
Once nuts are done roasted, put arugula in big bowl and put remaining salad ingredients in bowl. Top with dressing (olive oil, balsamin, stevia, salt, and whatever else sounds good!)
notes: We ended up adding a pomegranate walnut spread which was in our fridge. Toss salad and serve with bread.

title: Spicy Magic Green Sauce
steps: Put everything in the food processor and blend. If needed, add more water to thin it out. Adjust seasonings to taste - i.e. more salt, pepper, vinegar, lime juice, jalapeno, or whatever else you want to toss in there to get the flavor you want.
notes: Honestly came out tasting extremely flavorful - particularly from the large amount of fresh herbs. Will only use 1 or 2 herbs next time instead of 3.

title: Hodge Podge Quinoa Bowl with White Beans and Kalamata Olives
steps: Cook quinoa.
In a large pan, place quinoa, kale and white beans and heat it up.
After it's heated, top it with finely minced kalamata olives and any other veggies you may want to use for a raw garnish - we used radishes and green onion.
Prep the dressing by blending lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and red pepper flakes until you have a creamy yellow sauce.
Drizzle that sauce and hit it with some pepper.
notes: Option to sprinkle some vegan cheese and get real funky with it.

title: Carrot and Broccoli Pad Thai
ingredientList: Lime Wedges for garnish plus 1 lime for sauce
steps: Preheat oven to 400. Press the tofu (if needed - depends on what kind you got - the high protein tofu doesn't need to be pressed)
Chop the broccoli, thinly slice the carrots, chop the scallions, mince the garlic, pick the cilantro
Cut tofu into squares, cover it in cornstarch, garlic powder, salt, pepper. Put on parchment paper on baking sheet. Make sure tofu squares are not touching. Cook for 30 minutes.
Using a steamer basket, steam the carrot and the broccoli.
Cook the pad thai noodles. Once finished cooking, lightly drizzle with sesame oil.
Using an immersion blender, blend peanut butter, juice of one lime, tamarind paste, coco aminos, tamari, rice vinegar, apple cider vinegar, coconut sugar, and garlic
Put pad thai noodles on a plate, top with steamed carrots, broccoli, and tofu. Then spoon the pad thai sauce on top of it all. Garnish with scallions, cilantro, peanuts, and lime wedges.

title: Tofu Ricotta
steps: Crumble up the tofu
Roughly chop the garlic
Juice the lemon
Place everything in food processor and blend
Adjust seasoning to taste

title: Healthy Vegan Chai Latte
ingredientList: 3-4 HEAPING wooden spoonfuls (using the wooden spoon that comes in the tin)
Stevia, Agave, Maple Syrup, Coconut sugar, etc.
steps: Boil a cup of water

Fill your mug 3/4 of the way with the hot boiled water, then add in chai powder and mix
*Exception to this rule is if you're using full fat coconut milk or other thick milk, then use more water
Fill the rest of the way with nut milk, and add sweetener to taste
Optional - use milk frother for extra yumminess

title: Red Beans and Rice
steps: Make beans lol

title: Carrot Peanut Butter Soup
ingredientList: 2-3 cups depending on how thick you want
steps: If you haven't already, cook quinoa and set aside.
Peel and chop carrots. Chop onion. Mince garlic and ginger.
Saute onion (medium heat) for a few minutes. add the carrots, garlic, and ginger and saute for 5 minutes
Add veggie broth and bring to boil. Once boiling, lower to simmer and simmer for 20 min or until carrots are soft enough to pierce with a fork.

Lower heat. Add peanut butter, miso, sriracha, chili flake, tofu and blend with immersion blender. Add water to your desired thickness of soup (if you're adding a lot of water, quickly heat it in electric kettle before you add it in so soup stays hot)
Add quinoa and garnish with cilantro

title: Garlic Tahini Sauce
steps: Combine all ingredients in a jar and shake or whisk until it's smooth.
notes: I used salt, pepper, herbs de provence, and oregano to season but this can be experimented with

title: Chickpea Sandwich
steps: Mash up the chickpeas
Dice all the veggies
Mix the veganaise, hot sauce, vinegar, mustard, herbs and spices, lemon juice in a bowl.
If you want to toast the bread, do it- otherwise just make the sandwich and top it with lettuce and tomato
notes: It's really hard to mess up this recipe so try different ingredients and improvise with what you have. If you don't have celery, no worries, if you want to mix in more/less sauce, go for it.

title: Quick Easy Chili
steps: Dice up the onion, carrot, garlic, and peppers
Saute the veggies until they're soft and then add 2 cups of broth, the beans, and spices and mix it all together

If you're using an instant pot, simply pour the tomato sauce and diced tomatoes on top and close the lid, then pressure cook for 10 minutes (and also use 10 minute natural release method).
If not, simply add the tomato sauce and diced tomatoes and let the mixture simmer for at least a couple hours.
notes:
This recipe is super easy to make with an instant pot but not necessary.
The instant pot makes it cook faster but you're also constrained by how much can fit into the pot - if you need to cook a lot, then just make it on the stove top in a large pot.

title: Delicata Squash Lime Soup with Hominy and Cilantro Oil
steps: Preheat oven to 400. Slice ends off delicata squash, then scoop out seeds. Place squash in a baking pan face down with some water. Cook 30-45 minutes (cook till the squash are tender enough to scoop the insides out of the skin easily)
Make cilantro oil - place the bunch of cilantro in food processor (stems are fine) with 1/2 cup of olive oil and juice from 1/2 of a lime. Blend together very very very well - then strain out solids.

In a large soup pot - saute onions, carrots, and celery till tender using just a small amount of high heat oil. Add veggie broth, juice from 1/2 lime, coriander, and salt. Scoop out the cooked squash and add it in. Cover with lid and simmer 10 minutes. Then use immersion blender to blend it all up. You may want to add a little more lime or seasoning to the soup - so do a taste test at this point (don't forget the flavor will change a bit once you add cilantro oil).

Heat hominy in a separate pan or in oven - however you want! Once hominy is warm, add hominy to soup, season with a small amount of black pepper, and then drizzle with cilantro oil for garnish and flavahhh.
notes: Soup may initially seem a little too "limey" when plain - but adding the cilantro oil really balances out the flavor. Make sure you use 2 full teaspoons of salt - no more no less.

title: Palak Paneer
ingredientList: 5 cups or about 6 oz (about 1/3 of a big plastic box of spinach)
1-2 blocks (depending on how much paneer you want)
2 medium (I prefer yellow heirloom tomatoes over red for flavor)
1-2 cups dry (depending on how much rice you want)
Spices: Curry powder, cumin, and garam masala
a few tablespoons or enough for hearty sprinkling to make the paneer extra cheesy
steps: Cook brown rice in instant pot.
Press tofu. As tofu is pressing, preheat oven to 425.
Prep veggies - dice up the onion, garlic, serrano pepper, tomato. Finely grate ginger.

Heat up large pan on medium heat. As pan is heating up, cube the tofu and coat it in coconut aminos, nutritional yeast, some big dashes of curry powder, and salt. Put the tofu on parchment paper (keep each piece of tofu separate) on a baking sheet and bake in oven for 20 minutes.

Now that your pan has heated up, add high heat oil and onion. Saute until onion softens up. Then add serrano pepper, garlic, ginger, tomato, a few small dashes of cumin, a few bigger dashes of garam masala. Saute about 5 minutes or till it feels right to you (don't forget, you will be blending everything in a blender later).

While veggies saute, in electric kettle boil a large amount of water (fill kettle to the max). Once boiling, pour the hot water over the spinach in a large bowl. Let sit for about 30 seconds, then drain.

In a blender, add the sauteed veggies, wilted spinach, coconut milk, and Thai Kitchen Green Curry Paste. Add more curry paste to taste. Add coconut aminos (or tamari/liquid aminos) to taste. Add extra spices to taste (curry powder, cumin powder, garam masala, etc). Add salt as needed.
Serve it up! Brown rice, sauce, and tofu.
notes:
If fresh tomatoes are not available, you can sub a can of diced tomatoes.
You can also try adding some tamari/liquid aminos to this recipe.

title: Curry Lentil Soup
steps: Chop/Dice/Grate the carrots, ginger, potatoes, and onion. Get a saute pan warmed up with oil and then saute the onion, ginger, garlic for a few minutes. Then add the curry powder and cayenne powder and continue to saute for another 1-3 minutes.

Add veggie stock, lentils, and the rest of the veggies. Bring to a boil and then reduce temp to simmer and cover for around 30 minutes. This should give time for lentils to cook through and carrots and potatoes to soften. May need to add more water or broth.

Once the lentils, carrots, and potatoes have cooked through, throw in the spinach. I also like adding some peas if I have them around. At this point you also add the coconut milk. Get everything hot and take off heat.
notes:
Measurements are approximations. I used a mixture of garam masala powder and regular yellow curry powder combined with some cayenne and it was delicious. Also - using frozed chopped spinach is super convenient.

Also use whatever veggies sound good to you- cauliflower would probably be great. I've used cherry pepper for some tasty heat.
Enjoyed with a base of black rice which was delicious.

title: Lazy Black Bean Soup
steps: Chop/Dice the celery, carrot, onion, jalapeno, and garlic
Saute the veggies for 4-5 minutes - then add the spices. I also usually throw in a mixture of onion powder, garlic powder, some oregano, marjoram - whatever you're feeling.
After veggies have cooked with the spices for a minute or two, add the tomato, beans, corn, and broth
Keep the temperature high until you see some bubbles on the surface - then reduce to a simmer and cook for another 15-30 minutes.
notes: This recipe is super easy because you can really use whatever you have and just eyeball measurements - no precision needed at all - just don't add too much smoked paprika. Also hold off on the jalapeno if you don't want spice.

title: Tomato
ingredientList: Whatever you want - paprika, garlic, onion, oregano, marjoram, etc.
steps: In a giant soup pot - saute onions and carrots for a few minutes to soften them up
Add veggie broth - bring to a boil then quickly lower to a simmer and cook till the onion and carrot are soft
Add cans of diced tomatoes, garlic, spices, salt, pepper, maple syrup, and cashew butter - cook for a few more minutes
With your immersion blender - blend it all up
Top it off with several basil leaves and enjoy
